data.title: Blue Sky Uranium launches $1.02M private placement offering
data.description:
• Blue Sky Uranium (TSXV:BSK:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to $1.02M.
  • The offering includes up to 17M units priced at $0.06/unit.
  • Each unit comprises one common share and one warrant to buy another share at $0.075 within 36 months.
  • Proceeds will be used for general working capital purposes.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Bulgaria set to adopt euro next year after EU nod
data.description:
Bulgaria is one step closer to adopting the euro as its currency next year, as it received approval from the European Commission and European Central Bank after meeting certain economic criteria.

Joining the Eurozone will bring long-term benefits including a higher credit rating, more investments, promotion of trade and tourism, and greater confidence in the Bulgarian economy, Prime Minister Rosen Zhelyazkov told local media.

Bulgaria joined the European Union in 2007 and had been working towards adopting the euro. But its efforts to adopt the euro had stalled due to persistent inflationary pressures and political unrest.

The European Commission and ECB both said that Bulgaria met the criteria for euro adoption, including price stability and public finances. Next, EU leaders will discuss the matter before final approval, with the currency conversion rate likely to be set by the bloc's finance ministers next month.

While no opposition within the EU is expected, many Bulgarians are worried that adopting the euro could lead to higher prices.

"Ensuring price transparency and combating abusive price increases will require a special effort," said European Trade Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis.

data.title: Wells Fargo sets expansion aspirations after asset cap lifted - report
data.description:
Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) aims to build up corporate and investment bank and expand its branded credit-card business, among its list of plans to grow the bank after seven years of being constrained by a $1.95T asset cap, according to a media report published on Thursday.

CEO Charlie Scharf sees a path to become a top five investment bank, he told the Wall Street Journal in an interview. The company also is exploring using its balance sheet to enter the fast-growing business of direct lending, or financing of midsized companies and leveraged buyouts, he said.

After the close of market on Tuesday, the Federal Reserve removed the asset cap, placed on the company in 2018 due to a raft of scandals over dodgy sales practices and compliance failures, and terminated the associated consent order against the bank. While the cap was in place, Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) had turned down deposits at its commercial bank, rather than turn away consumer or wealth deposits, Scharf said last week at a conference. 

With a freer rein now, the company will also promote to its wealth management clients other services at the bank. It's also considering whether to originate home equity loans after it pausing the business, Scharf said.

Wells Fargo (WFC) stock ticked up 0.1% in premarket trading.

data.title: Buckle reports 7.2% rise in comparable store sales for May
data.description:
• The Buckle (NYSE:BKE) Thursday said that comparable store net sales, for stores open at least one year, for the 4-week period ended May 31, increased 7.2%.
  • Net sales for the 4-week fiscal month ended May 31 increased 7.8% to $88.4 million from net sales of $82.0 million for the prior year 4-week fiscal month ended June 1, 2024.

data.title: MarketAxess reports 44% higher ADV in May
data.description:
Electronic trading platform MarketAxess (NASDAQ:MKTX) announced on Thursday a trading average daily volume of $46.23B in May, up 44% from the prior-year period and down 20% month-over-month.

Credit ADV rose 22% on a yearly basis and fell 11% from the previous month to $16.35B. Rates ADV increased 59% year-over-year and declined 24% from a month ago to $29.88B.

Eurobonds ADV reached $2.9B on a 116% increase in block trading and a 500% increase in portfolio trading ADV.

data.title: Avricore appoints Rodger Seccombe as CEO
data.description:
• Avricore (AVCR:CA) Thursday named Rodger Seccombe as CEO of the company. 
  • Seccombe currently serves as Chief Technology Officer of Avricore and is the President and Co-Founder of its wholly owned subsidiary, HealthTab.
  • Hector Bremner has resigned from his position as CEO and director of the company.

data.title: Networking stocks mixed after Ciena's mixed Q1 results
data.description:
• Networking stocks were mixed in premarket trading on Thursday after Ciena (NYSE:CIEN) reported mixed first-quarter results compared to Wall Street's forecast.
  • Ciena shares fell 5.5% in premarket trading, as the Gary Smith-led company earned an adjusted $0.42 per share during the period, $0.10 worse than the consensus estimate. However, revenue for the period jumped 24.1% year-over-year to $1.13B, $40M above the consensus. Sales for the quarter were driven by two customers, which accounted for 23.9% of total revenue. 
  • Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) bucked the decline and rose 0.4% in premarket trading, while Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO) fell 0.1%. Juniper Networks (NYSE:JNPR), which is still in the process of being acquired by HP Enterprise (HPE), was little changed.
  • Ciena is set to host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Flow Beverage CFO steps down
data.description:
• Flow Beverage (TSX:FLOW:CA) (OTCQX:FLWBF) said that Trent MacDonald has stepped down from his position as CFO and executive VP of Operations.
  • The Board of Directors has initiated a process to select a new CFO and is in advanced discussions with a preferred candidate for this position.
  • In the interim, the company’s Director of Finance will perform similar functions to those of the CFO. 
  • Mr. MacDonald will assist with this transition before he moves.

data.title: European markets in green as investors await ECB interest rate decision
data.description:
Update: The revised post updates market movements as of 7.20am ET. 

London (UKX) +0.31% to 8,828, after trading almost flat for three days as mixed corporate news weigh investor sentiment.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.29% to 24,348, surpassing 24,300 to hit a new milestone and extend its winning streak to three consecutive days. The HCOB Germany Construction PMI slipped to 44.4 in May. Factory orders in Germany rose 0.6% M/M in April.

France (CAC:IND) +0.37% to 7,833, The HCOB Construction PMI in France fell to 43.1 in May.

The HCOB Eurozone Construction PMI fell to 45.6 in May.

The HCOB Italy Construction PMI edged up to 50.5 in May.

Finland’s trade deficit narrowed to €0.10B in April. Slovenia posted a trade deficit of €446.5M in April.

Sweden’s current account surplus widened sharply to SEK 119.3B in Q1.

The Swiss unemployment rate remained stable at a non-seasonally adjusted 2.8% in May.

Wholesale prices in Austria fell by 0.5% Y/Y in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) gained 0.36% to 553, as investors refrained from making big bets ahead of the European Central Bank’s monetary policy decision later in the day. European natural gas futures climbed above €36 per megawatt-hour as supply from Norway, the region’s top gas exporter, dropped due to planned maintenance at the Kollsnes processing plant.

Coming up in the session: ECB interest rate decision is lined up for the day, where it expected to cut interest rates by another 25 basis points at its June meeting as it seeks to support growth amid slowing inflation and ongoing global trade uncertainties.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down basis point to 4.35%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 2.49%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 4.58%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Dine Brands prices $600M senior notes financing
